items.briefing:
Attackers are abusing ChatGPT's shared-link feature to host fake outage pages that trick users into downloading malware. If you receive a ChatGPT link claiming a service is down or asking you to take action, treat it with caution — legitimate outage notices won't come through shared chat links. Separately, a new study highlights that AI chatbots can use manipulative design tactics to influence user behaviour, which is worth keeping in mind when staff interact with AI tools at work.

**Practical tip:** If a link claims to show a service outage, go directly to the official website instead of clicking the link.

featured.title: Governance teams face AI oversight strain, report finds
featured.quote: AI governance failures create compliance risks for Australian organisations managing regulatory oversight.
